issue_title: Problematic shebangs in example files.
issue_body:
`#!/usr/bin/python` does not work well with virtualenvs. You could use `#!/usr/bin/env python`.

To reproduce, create a fresh virtualenv, source it, do a `python setup.py build install`, try to run any example, you'll see that googleads package can't be found. Thats because its using the system's python interpreter instead of the virtualenv one. My suggestion is either change it or remove execution permissions on the files and force people to call it with `python <path_to_example.py` so it would use the correct python interpreter.

issue_title: Pushing an empty UrlList has no effect
issue_body:
We are currently migrating our product to the new destination URLs and are hitting a snag. Setting finalUrls and finalMobilUrls on regular keywords works fine, but after setting URLs it is impossible to clear the list. This is the dictionary that we are passing to the mutate method:

    {'operand': {'adGroupId': '<redacted>',
              'criterion': {'id': '<redacted>',
                            'matchType': u'EXACT',
                            'text': u'some keyword',
                            'xsi_type': 'Keyword'},
              'destinationUrl': '',
              'finalMobileUrls': {'urls': []},
              'finalUrls': {'urls': []},
              'xsi_type': 'BiddableAdGroupCriterion'},
  'operator': 'SET'} 

The following XML blob is created by suds:

    <?xml version="1.0" ?>
    <SOAP-ENV:Envelope xmlns:SOAP-ENV="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:ns0="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:ns1="https://adwords.google.com/api/adwords/cm/v201502" xmlns:tns="https://adwords.google.com/api/adwords/cm/v201502" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
        <SOAP-ENV:Header>
            <tns:RequestHeader>
                <tns:clientCustomerId>REDACTED</tns:clientCustomerId>
                <tns:developerToken>REDACTED</tns:developerToken>
                <tns:userAgent>(AwApi-Python, googleads/3.4.1, Python/2.7)</tns:userAgent>
                <tns:validateOnly>false</tns:validateOnly>
                <tns:partialFailure>false</tns:partialFailure>
            </tns:RequestHeader>
        </SOAP-ENV:Header>
        <ns0:Body>
            <ns1:mutate>
                <ns1:operations>
                    <ns1:operator>SET</ns1:operator>
                    <ns1:operand xsi:type="ns1:BiddableAdGroupCriterion">
                        <ns1:adGroupId>REDACTED</ns1:adGroupId>
                        <ns1:criterion xsi:type="ns1:Keyword">
                            <ns1:id>REDACTED</ns1:id>
                            <ns1:Criterion.Type>Keyword</ns1:Criterion.Type>
                            <ns1:text>some keyword</ns1:text>
                            <ns1:matchType>EXACT</ns1:matchType>
                        </ns1:criterion>
                        <ns1:AdGroupCriterion.Type>BiddableAdGroupCriterion</ns1:AdGroupCriterion.Type>
                        <ns1:destinationUrl/>
                    </ns1:operand>
                </ns1:operations>
            </ns1:mutate>
        </ns0:Body>
    </SOAP-ENV:Envelope>


In this XML blob the new (empty) UrlLists for finalUrls and finalMobileUrls are missing, and hence they are not cleared in AdWords. Setting the urls key to None or the finalUrls to {} or None doesn't solve the problem. It appears that either the adwords client or suds is removing the empty object.

issue_title: Don't embed hooks
issue_body:
Currently, layered charms' hooks are an embedded chunk of Python. This might come from the base layer, or an old version of the base layer, or customized by an included layer, or customized by the main charm. This makes it difficult to propagate fixes, and is a bit of a mess.

Instead, lets not have any hooks in the hooks directory. List the hooks that each layer needs in its layers.yaml file. At composition time, copy hooks/hook.template into place for each hook and set executable permissions, if the hook does not already exist. 

This would allow changes in the base layer's hook.template to propagate. It would allow layers and charms to create a new hook.template if necessary. It would allow layers and charms to override the hook template just for those hooks that actually need it.

issue_title: Configuration stored as connection objects instead of on module singleton
issue_body:
Configurations are stored as global state on the `Configuration` module, example:

`Configuration.create(hdx_site='prod')`

I would prefer if Configurations were individually instantiated as a "connection" object. This would make application code easier to unit test and more decoupled. A good example is the AWS Boto API e.g.  
>>> from boto.s3.connection import S3Connection
>>> conn = S3Connection('<aws access key>', '<aws secret key>')

issue_title: add support for 6PE [RFC4798]
issue_body:
I see issue #382 was open and closed, but I'm not clear that RFC4798 is supported in exabgp

family "ipv4 nlri-mpls"  is supported, but family "ipv6 nlri-mpls" is not valid

I would expect "ipv6 nlri-mpls" to be AFI=2, SAFI=4, labeled IPv6 (6PE)

Am I missing something?  

I would like to see ipv6 labeled unicast supported

Thanks

**Describe the bug**
The tutorial `using_as_a_library_pt1.md` has a broken link. It's meant to link to `using_as_a_library_pt2.md` but instead links to `making_predictions_and_creating_a_demo.md`.

Steps to reproduce the behavior

3. Click on **Making Predictions and Creating a Demo**

The link should instead take you to [part 2](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/tutorials/getting_started/using_as_a_library_pt2.md) of the tutorial.

issue_title: `ModelTestCase.ensure_model_can_train_save_and_load` compares last-epoch weights with serialized *best* weights
issue_body:
in particular, if your test case model trained (say) 10 epochs, and got the best results in epoch 5, then the test would fail because it would compare `model.state_dict()` (i.e. epoch 10) with `archive.model.state_dict()` (the weights from epoch 5)

in the meantime I am setting `num_epoch = 1` as a workaround

issue_title: memory collectors uses wrong property to get actual memory available
issue_body:
According to documentation (http://pythonhosted.org/psutil/#psutil.virtual_memory) *free* isn't the right property to read amount of available memory - https://github.com/python-diamond/Diamond/blob/master/src/collectors/memory/memory.py#L115

```
free: memory not being used at all (zeroed) that is readily available; note that this doesn’t reflect the actual memory available (use ‘available’ instead).
```

issue_body:
Calling show('braille') on any object fails from the standard Python interpreter (rather than using a notebook).  For example:

>>> n=note.Note()
>>> n.show('braille')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "C:\Users\Alastair\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python36-32\lib\site-pac
kages\music21\base.py", line 2578, in show
    **keywords)
  File "C:\Users\Alastair\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python36-32\lib\site-pac
kages\music21\converter\subConverters.py", line 461, in show
    super().show(obj, fmt, app=None, subformats=None, **keywords)
  File "C:\Users\Alastair\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python36-32\lib\site-pac
kages\music21\converter\subConverters.py", line 182, in show
    returnedFilePath = self.write(obj, fmt, subformats=subformats, **keywords)
  File "C:\Users\Alastair\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python36-32\lib\site-pac
kages\music21\converter\subConverters.py", line 470, in write
    if 'ascii' in subformats:
TypeError: argument of type 'NoneType' is not iterable
>>>

Looking at the code I see two issues.  Firstly the line which causes the above failure should probably be changed to include if subformats is not None.  Fixing this the above example works for me.  Secondly, in the show method of the BrailleConverter class, when ipython is not being used, the super call should probably pass the value of subformats to the parent rather than None.  This is needed so that show('braille.ascii') works correctly.

issue_title: Add support for JSON text sequences
issue_body:
:rfc:`7464` specifies a (fairly simple) format for sequences of JSON values, an alternative to having a giant array of values.

It's got support already in a bunch of places, including within the Python ecosystem (e.g. it is the output format of `twisted.logger`).

For comparison, e.g. jq already supports `jq --seq` to process JSON seqs.

issue_title: avg function throws ZeroDivisionError
issue_body:
According to avg function specifications (http://jmespath.org/specification.html#avg) - _"An empty array will produce a return value of null."_
Instead, for json like this:
```json
{"data": [1,2,3,4], "zero_data": []}
```
jmespath `datum | avg(@)` throws an Exception:
```python
lib/python2.7/site-packages/jmespath/functions.pyc in _func_avg(self, arg)
    177     @builtin_function({'types': ['array-number']})
    178     def _func_avg(self, arg):
--> 179         return sum(arg) / float(len(arg))
    180
    181     @builtin_function({'types': [], 'variadic': True})

ZeroDivisionError: float division by zero
```

issue_title: proposal feat(client) allow for custom git hostname
issue_body:
When I was trying to set cloudflare between deis and rest of the world it came to my attention that they will block anything except http/https traffic (for example tcp/2222), I don't know if cloudfront is affected too. 

Maybe I can add support for optional git hostname which will be passed from commandline to client.json, and used for all git related actions. I could implement it myself client code looks clean. 

I just need your opinion.

issue_title: HTTP 413 (entity too large) error after setting bodySize to "10000M"
issue_body:
I have an AWS deis deployment (1.11.2) and getting a 413 http error when trying to upload a 355 MB video. The request doesn't make it to my app as it is not shown in my app logs.

I tried

```
deisctl config router set bodySize=10000M
```

but still getting the 413 error. I tried to check the docker logs of deis-router but couldn't find anything related to that.

issue_title: Add type hints for all packages
issue_body:
[Type hints](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0484/) allow us to perform static type checking, among other things. We could benefit a lot from using them.

[This stackoverflow answer](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/32557920/what-are-type-hints-in-python-3-5) does a great job at describing their main benefits.

[pyannotate](https://github.com/dropbox/pyannotate) would come in handy if it had support for py3-style annotations, but meanwhile we can add them manually

[monkeytype](https://engineering.instagram.com/let-your-code-type-hint-itself-introducing-open-source-monkeytype-a855c7284881) may actually be more useful than pyannotate as it generates py3-style annotations

### Definition of done

- type hints for all of `evm`, `p2p`, and `trinity` modules.
- individual `mypy` CI runs for each of these modules using `mypy --follow-imports=silent --ignore-missing-imports --check-untyped-defs --disallow-incomplete-defs -p <module>`

This should not be done as a single pull request, but rather many small ones to iteratively add type hints, slowly expanding the `mypy` coverage as hints are added.

issue_title: Add IPython Support To Default Py-EVM shell
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Nothing is wrong with current flow. This will be an improvement to current Py-EVM lightchain_shell.py module interactive mode shell.

IPython is very widely used Python REPL. It provides tab completion/hinting, command history, command autocomplete, history navigation and many such handy features that help in development.

### How can it be fixed

lightchain_shell.py file can be modified to add support for running shell from the code. By default run the IPython shell if it is installed on the current PYTHONPATH. If an import error is raised run the default Python REPL with improvements like tab completion and history navigation through up key.

issue_title: MRS codecs should use the Mrs() constructor
issue_body:
There's an asymmetry between the mrs() and eds() methods on ParseResults:

```python
>>> r = rest.parse('Dogs bark.', params={'mrs':'simple', 'eds':'native'})
>>> r.result(0).eds().to_dict()
{ ... }
>>> r.result(0).mrs().to_dict()
...
AttributeError: 'Xmrs' object has no attribute 'to_dict'
```

This is because the underlying codec for mrs(), simplemrs, instantiates Xmrs instead of Mrs(). Changing this should allow the ParseResult calls to be symmetric, and is probably the better thing to do anyway. Similarly, check that the other codecs instantiate their respective classes instead of the generic one.

issue_title: `mkprof` has incompatibilities with Python2
issue_body: The `mkprof` command catches PermissionError, a subclass of OSError, but it is not available in Python2.

issue_title: Test for virtualenv fails for conda env
issue_body:
Can someone help me understand how conda envs differ from those created by `virtualenv`? 

I ran across [this code](https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil/blob/master/Makefile#L185) that checks for an active virtualenv,
```
check_venv:
        @$(python) -c 'import sys; sys.exit( int( not hasattr(sys, "real_prefix") ) )' \
                || ( printf "$(red)A virtualenv must be active.$(normal)" ; false )
```

I have no idea why this requirement is enforced but this check fails if using conda to manage python environments.

I would like to modify this test to work with conda env as well.

issue_title: Performance improvement for sending requests
issue_body:
The Capi team discovered a 40 ms delay that happens on subsequent requests to a keepalive connection between when the client sends its headers and when it sends the rest of the data (this is due to an interaction of a deferred ACK on the server side and nagle's algorithm on the client side). This can be addressed by sending the data in the endheaders request.

It would also be nice to have a benchmark of the pyeapi library so that we can see the performance improvement of this change.

issue_title: Support controllers and responders.
issue_body:
Currently only members are stored which are all contollers and responders.  We need a way to identify them.

The xml looks like:
```
<group flag="132">
<address>30785</address>
<name>Family Room Table</name>
<parent type="3">52063</parent>
<deviceGroup>23</deviceGroup>
<ELK_ID>C02</ELK_ID>
<members>
<link type="16">21 8F 5B 1</link>
<link type="16">1D FF E9 5</link>
<link type="16">1C 46 CB 1</link>
<link type="32">33 1 49 6</link>
<link type="32">2A E 4D 8</link>
</members>
</group>
``

Where 32 is a responder and 16 is a controller.

Ryan requested:

I would prefer to have the members array only be responders and then a new array called controllers be created. Good catch with that. I like UDI, but their documentation on that REST API is terribly flaky. Looking at that type number, I'd bet dollars to donuts that there is also an 8, 4, 2, and 1. They probably then treat those as 6 bit binary flags. So:
100000 - Responder (32)
010000 - Controller (16)
001000 - Something (8)
000100 - Something (4)
000010 - Something (2)
000001 - Something (1)
000000 - ISY System Level Pairing, Not a true Insteon pairing (this is my best guess)

My guess is that they used this design so that nodes could belong to multiple categories. For us, however, I don't think we need to worry about this right now. Let's just make sure that 0, 16, and 32 are handled. I think 0 should be treated like 32 for now.

issue_title: SSL client auth problem in version 5.5.1
issue_body:
With version `5.5.1` it is not possible to use SSL client auth (`client_cert` and `client_key`) when connection with `verify_certs=False`.

As [we can see in the code](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/5.5.1/elasticsearch/connection/http_urllib3.py#L76) if  we set  `verify_certs=False` then ssl client auth will be ignored. 

Also this behaviuor is undocumented, see [`5.5.1` docs](http://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.io/en/5.5.1/connection.html#urllib3httpconnection-default-connection-class).

But in version `6.0.0` this configuration is possible ([see code](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/6.0.0/elasticsearch/connection/http_urllib3.py#L107) ).

The changes was introduced in `6.0.0` with [this commit](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/commit/7339a272b58536921cc6a4f50d0aa12c67483566#diff-e03df718a08bdad9d78d94c50dd7db90).

Nevertheless the behaviour introduced in `6.0.0` seems correct, or at least removes an undocumented feature and permit a more flexible usage of the library, and should be backported also `5.x` releases.

Here is what happens when I run the command on the bash cloud shell: `morgan@Azure:~/clouddrive/quickstart/nodejs-docs-hello-world$ az webapp up -n mg18sampleapp2` [omitted for brevity] `[Errno 22] Invalid argument: '/usr/morgan/clouddrive/quickstart\nodejs-docs-hello-world.zip'` Note the backslash instead of a forward slash in the path. **To Reproduce** Follow this tutorial: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/containers/quickstart-nodejs

issue_title: setup: *.pyc get regenerated from *.py files on code start
issue_body:
With #6309 merged which excludes pyc files from the installer, it might not be a good use of our time to investigate this, but i still log it with a bit detail for reference in case we like to bring pyc files back, though we should also consider to only including *.pyc file for smaller installer file size.
Repro:
1. Create a Windows VM: `az vm create -g rg -n winvm --image MicrosoftWindowsDesktop:Windows-10:RS3-Pro:latest --admin-username clitester --admin-password verrverySecret12! --size Standard_DS2_v2`
2. Install CLI `https://azurecliprod.blob.core.windows.net/msi/azure-cli-2.0.31.msi`
3. Under `C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft SDKs\Azure\CLI2\Lib\site-packages\azure\cli\core\__pycache__` you should see file stamp at `Apr 30, 2018` which is when we cut out the installer
4. Invoke the command window under admin, so python can re-generate the pyc file w/o permission issue
5. run `az` and go back to program files folder and you should see pyc files have new time stamps like below 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6014209/40216356-01412534-5a1b-11e8-9f00-a243b327a2e1.png)

issue_title: Azure Cloud Shell improvements
issue_body:
### Description

## Login
While users are automatically logged in, the 'az login' command should still be supported, but not default to a device login.  

Currently it does:
```
jason@Azure:~$ az login
To sign in, use a web browser to open the page https://aka.ms/devicelogin and enter the code BN3K7K4R2 to authenticate.
```

I propose

```
jason@Azure:~$ az login
The Azure Cloud Shell has automatically logged you in.
Type "az login --force" to log in anyway

Your current subscription is: Azure CLI Demo with TTL = 2 days
Type "az account list" to show other available subscriptions

issue_title: Cosmosdb create collection with custom partition key always fails
issue_body:
Using the command:

az cosmosdb collection create --name <redacted> --db-name <redacted> --resource-group <redacted> --collection-name <redacted> --key <redacted> --partition-key-path "/StoreUid" 

This command results in an error including:

DEBUG: Status code: 400
{"code":"BadRequest","message":"Message: {\"Errors\":[\"The 'kind' value '' specified in the partition key definition is invalid. Please choose between 'Hash' and 'Range' partition 
types.\"]}\r\


ERROR: Operation Failed: Invalid Arg {"Errors":["The 'kind' value '' specified in the partition key definition is invalid. Please choose between 'Hash' and 'Range' partition types."]}

Upon investigation I noticed that the request is malformed and missing the the kind value in command_modules, azure-cli-cosmosdb, azure, cli, command_modules, cosmosdb, custom. in def _populate_collection_definition

issue_title: Commerce: Rate Card "meter_sub_category" output format has changed!
issue_body:
Was wondering why my code for outputting VM price started failing. Found out the "meter_sub_category" for the Rate Card meter object has changed.  For instance D8v3 use to have output:

Standard_D8_v3 VM

Now it outputs 

Dv3/DSv3 Series

This is problematic because compute_client.virtual_machine_sizes.list() gives the first format (Standard_D8_v3)

What an easy way to match up VM Size with the Rate card pricing?

issue_title: Query: Is there any api for creating a convex hull with set of points and ask for collision infos
issue_body:
Hi,
I am trying to implement a motion planner from this [paper](http://www.eecs.berkeley.edu/~joschu/docs/trajopt-paper.pdf), where collision information are need so that Jacobian can be used to avoid collision during planning.

Further, in this paper says that the discrete samples of a trajectory is not collision free in continuous time,  so we need to find the closest point between the convex hull made by A(t) and A(t+1) to have a collision free trajectory in continuous time.

So, my query is that, is there any way to form a convexhull between set of points in a trajectory and call getClosestPoint from this created convexhull.

Thanks in advance ..

MacOS homebrew installer - request for update with support for pybullet
issue_body:
I'm trying to update the [Homebrew/homebrew-core](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core) repository with [this new bullet.rb](https://github.com/ahundt/homebrew-core/blob/bullet/Formula/bullet.rb) install script, which is modified to support pybullet [with these changes](https://github.com/ahundt/homebrew-core/commit/fd915407ba908b709f5d711a4529ac0bac8d9320) in a [ahundt/homebrew-core bullet branch](https://github.com/ahundt/homebrew-core/tree/bullet), but there appears to be bugs in bullet's own CMake script for setting Python and NumPy variables on OSX, triggering CMake build error and selecting the incorrect python library version.

I attempt to install with the following command:
```
brew uninstall bullet --ignore-dependencies; brew install bullet --with-demo --with-double-precision --with-framework -v
```

The formula (aka ruby install script) repository should be in the `/usr/local/Homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-core/Formula` folder if brew is installed.

My updated version of the script generates this cmake command:
```

issue_title: Feature Request: Fuzzy Finder for code suggestion tool (eg. pressing '.' then [tab] and begin typing)
issue_body:
Hi All, 

This is is a request for a better fuzzy autocompletion tool for jupyter notebooks (ipython).  

It would be a great help to have a simpler yet better fuzzy finder that made it easier to isolate functions relevant to a particular class.  

Currently the autocomplete for jupyter feels more like a spelling checker (you have to know what you are looking for, be able to spell it, and then scroll heavily to find it ).

I am coding using deep learning libraries which pytorch, keras, tensorflow, theano, and these have pretty convoluted and similar and potentially confusing APIs.  Fuzzy finding in these libraries would be a huge help.

eg some examples in keras API
keras.layers

e.g. typing .1d might give you all the operations:
- AvfPool**1D**
- AveragePooling**1D**
- Conv**1D**

e.g. type .cv2d might give you all the operations:
- **C**on**v2D**
- De**C**on**v2D**
- *but not* Cropping2D

For inspiration [Fuzzy finder](https://github.com/amjith/fuzzyfinder) is a python library which does this, it appears.
![fuzzy finder example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/amjith/fuzzyfinder/master/screenshots/pgcli-fuzzy.gif)

Is it possible to incorporate this code, or something similar, into an upcoming version of jupyter. It would be a great help!!

For other related discussions, I have searched the codebase and found these two issues:
[Better autocompletion for our Python APIs #135](https://github.com/googledatalab/datalab/issues/135)
[Fuzzy search completion list #504](https://github.com/jonathanslenders/python-prompt-toolkit/issues/504) and haven't found a solution for this.  

I'd love to hear if anyone has found any solutions to this, or people who might be able to add their upvotes, or other considerations.

Thanks

issue_title: latexing errors are opaque
issue_body:
(this report was related by @williamstein)

When the notebook does a conversion to pdf, it runs latex.  If there are any errors, the error message shown to the user is not very helpful and does not help pinpointing the error.  We could, for example, try to capture the error message from tex, run while ignoring tex errors so *something* is produced, or at least show the command line jupyter used to tex the file so the user can try to reproduce the problem.

issue_title: Add faq entry about installing on python without sqlite extensions
issue_body:
In https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/1310, we added a fallback if sqlite was not compiled into python: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/92d3f4235d9d5d591ca62e3b60ac9399315f5a65/notebook/services/sessions/sessionmanager.py#L12

In the issue this fixed, adding an item to the docs noting this extra dependency if sqlite was not included was mentioned, but not done (as far as I can tell): https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/1307#issuecomment-206580744

This would have probably helped us (at least a little) in resolving https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyterlab/issues/4425

issue_title: Default session for the TensorFlowModel
issue_body:
As I understood from the code (https://github.com/bethgelab/foolbox/blob/master/foolbox/models/tensorflow.py), the constructor of the TensorFlowModel tries to use the default session, otherwise it creates a new one, where all variables need to be reinitialised. That means that if there is a running session with initialised variables in it, then the TensorFlowModel should be created with this session being a default one:

```
with sess.as_default():
    model = foolbox.models.TensorFlowModel(...)
```

Maybe that is obvious, but it took me some time to figure what was going on, so you guys may consider adding a note on that to the documentation :-)

issue_title: scipy and hessian-vector-product
issue_body:
A very minor issue but thought I'd bring it up.

scipy.optimize.minimize expects hessian_vector_product(args, vector).

However, the hessian_vector_product in autograd returns a function like
hessian_vector_product(vector, args).

So for compatibility with scipy.optimize.minimize it would be more convenient to swap the order of the parameters.

But I am not so familiar with other optimizers in Python, and perhaps they expect the hessian_vector_product to be parametrized differently.

issue_title: The Resource `create` and `load` Aliasing Issue
issue_body:
`Resource` `create` and `load` create 2 aliases for the same object in the callers namespace, _IF_ they're called on the right hand side of an assignment statement.

This leads to confusing errors like this:

https://github.com/F5Networks/f5-common-python/issues/407

This "design" issue is being created to provide a single location to discuss the problem and come up with fix.

issue_title: Sync to group in cm failing to sync
issue_body:
Here's me issuing the command:

>>> from f5.bigip import BigIP
>>> b = BigIP('10.190.20.202', '<un>', '<pw>')
>>> b.refresh()
>>> b.cm.sync('test')

And the BigIP sync status does not update, nor does the 'Last Sync Time' on the device group. My proposed change is based on some info I found in a devcentral question. The suggested command to run to sync is as follows:  `curl -sk -u admin:admin -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -d '{"command":"run","utilCmdArgs":"config-sync to-group pair-group-name"}' https://x.x.x.x/mgmt/tm/cm`

I will implement that and also implement the `config-sync from-group' command as well. I have this change locally and it's working well.

issue_title: Template and Service resources in Applications should required partition as creation parameter
issue_body: The 'partition' should be a required_creation_parameter for the template and service resources.

issue_title: oscar.apps.offer.custom dependency on models prevents customization
issue_body:
The module `oscar.apps.offer.custom` imports the `Benefit` and `Condition` models directly instead of via `get_model()`. If either of these models is overridden by a forked version, this module cannot be imported.

The module should be updated to call `get_model()`.

issue_title: Http Client performance drop in preview-2
issue_body:
Hello. I was trying out HttpClient benchmarking for net core 2.1 and well, I am seeing a serious drop in the HttpClient provided in preview 2.

for simple Get requests between 2 Windows Server 2016 VMs set up with network acceleration in Azure, 
1. for Preview-1 with the "AppContext.SetSwitch("System.Net.Http.UseManagedHttpClientHandler", true);" I get 75,000 requests/second
2. For preview-2 using the SocketsHttpHandler, the performance drops to 43,000 requests/second

Is there a reason for this performance drop?

issue_title: Handle --redirect flag appropriately
issue_body:
The client does not properly handle the case when `--redirect` is specified on the command line. The root of the problem is this argument is treated as a binary variable in `cli.py` (`True` or `False`) and a ternary variable in `client.py` (`True`, `False`, or `None`). The resulting behavior is if `--redirect` is not specified on the command line, `client.py` assumes the user does not want a redirect.

The simple fix is to prompt the user on whether or not they want a redirect if `--redirect` is not included on the command line. A better fix is to allow the user to specify whether or not they want a redirect on the command line and prompting them if nothing is specified.

tw@tux:~/w/lets-encrypt-preview$ host myname.nsupdate.info
myname.nsupdate.info has IPv6 address 2002:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxff:fexx:xxxx (only v6 in DNS!)

tw@tux:~/w/lets-encrypt-preview$ host letsencrypt-demo.org
letsencrypt-demo.org has address 54.183.196.250 (only v4 in DNS!)

note: myname.nsupdate.info has v4 and v6 connectivity.

Same thing worked if I put an A record into DNS.

issue_title: source install of metakernel puts its data (logo files) to /usr/metakernel
issue_body:
Try e.g. `sudo pip install --upgrade .` from the source folder.  Note that the files dumped to the non-standard `/usr/metakernel` location don't even get removed upon `sudo pip uninstall metakernel`.

`package_data` is preferred to `data_files` nowadays (https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/2874#issuecomment-109444845), it should be possible to install the logos within the package folder instead.

Wheels also install to the non-standard `/usr/metakernel/`, but at least uninstall properly from there.

issue_title: byeaster and bysetpos can't be used together
issue_body:
Migrated from launchpad issue [#1402868](https://bugs.launchpad.net/dateutil/+bug/1402868):

Quoting [Thomas Roten](https://launchpad.net/~thomasroten-deactivatedaccount-deactivatedaccount) on 2014-12-15:
>I can create an rrule object like this:

    >>> bysetpos = rrule(freq=0, byweekday=6, bysetpos=-13)
    >>> bysetpos[0]
    datetime.datetime(2015, 10, 4, 14, 18, 5)

>I can also create another object like this:

    >>> byeaster = rrule(freq=0, byeaster=182)
    >>> byeaster[0]
    datetime.datetime(2015, 10, 4, 14, 17, 56)

>But, if I combine those rules, I get an exception:

    >>> easter_and_setpos = rrule(freq=0, byeaster=182, byweekday=6, bysetpos=-13)

issue_title: Opening the port in non-blocking and synchronization issues
issue_body:
**Situation: I have a device constantly feeding data to the serial port. When my Python program is not running, some data are buffered on the OS level (POSIX). When I open the serial port, I immediately get a chunk of the buffered data. I need to get rid of these...**

First I have tried:

```python
ser = serial.Serial('/dev/cu.usbserial-DN00BMJN', baudrate=115200)
ser.reset_input_buffer()
```

But it did not work (I was still immediately reading the buffered chunk of data). Then I found out that adding a small time delay helped:

```python
ser = serial.Serial('/dev/cu.usbserial-DN00BMJN', baudrate=115200)
time.sleep(0.5)
ser.reset_input_buffer()
```

Later I figured out it is because of the `O_NONBLOCK` flag passed to the `open` system call: https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/blob/master/serial/serialposix.py#L265

... In such case, the `open` call itself will not block and subsequent operations like flushing input buffers or determining the number of available bytes in the queue do not work.

Also, I can see that the library itself is attempting to flush the input buffers:

https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/blob/master/serial/serialposix.py#L295

... but this implementation does not work - or even worse - the execution result depends on the system performance (on slower systems, the open operation may have finished by the time reaching this call). From this reason, I believe it should be considered as a bug.

Please, consider opening the port synchronously, flushing the input buffers and then applying the `O_NONBLOCK` flag via the `fcntl` call. A different approach (and probably a better one) could be adding some synchronization mechanism at the end of the `open` method that will block until the device will be ready (unfortunately I do not know the exact POSIX implementation of this at the moment).

Thanks.

issue_title: Iterable links
issue_body:
Hi,

I think I've hot a bit of an issue with the links in my HAL documents. I think the correct place to raise this is in this project because my best guess is the solution will be found here rather than in the HAL code.

So I get this JSON structure returned:

```
{
  "_links": {
    "list": {
      "href": "/v1/pbx/extension?domainId=ORB1234567"
    },
    "relations": [
      {
        "href": "/v1/pbx/domain/id/ORB1234567",
        "name": "domain"
      },
      {
        "href": "/v1/pbx/device?domainId=ORB1234567",
        "name": "devices"
      }
    ],
    "self": {
      "href": "/v1/pbx/extension/domainId/ORB1234567/id/1000"
    }
  },
  "callidName": "The Manager",
  "callidNumber": "1000",
  "domainId": "ORB1234567",
  "email": "manager.user@some-domain.com",
  "firstName": "Manager",
  "id": "1000",
  "lastName": "User",
}
```

Which produces a copreapi.document.Document looking something like this when printed out:
```
<Document "https://rest.orbtalk.com/v1/pbx/extension/domainId/ORB1234567/id/1000">
    callidName: "The Manager"
    callidNumber: "1000"
    domainId: "ORB1234567"
    email: "manager.user@some-domain.com"
    firstName: "Manager"
    id: "1000"
    lastName: "User"
    relations: {
        devices()
        domain()
    }
    list()
```

So I can access the list() action with no problems. However I am having trouble calling the links inside my relations object. The HAL code seems to read it fine and it does detected the links correctly, however Document.links does not find those links because it does not look inside any iterable items when searching for links.

My suggestion is that I send in a PR that patches Document.links to look recursively inside each item for links. The slightly awkward issue then however is what they should be named. In my example above it seems reasonable to me that links might be created called "relations.domain" and "relations.devices". This gets more awkward though with lists of links that HAL does support - or nesting beyond a single level of depth.

An alternative approach could be a different way of being able to call links that are contained inside other items. I'm not sure if that might already be supported or if that would break the way the client is designed to work.

Any guidance that could be provided would be great. I'm more than happy to send in PR requests for review if that would help move this along.

issue_title: Solver parameter "timeout" doesn't behave correctly when setting timeout of zero
issue_body:
The documentation for the solver parameter "timeout" says

```
timeout (unsigned int) timeout (in milliseconds) (0 means no timeout) (default: 0)
```

However if I try and set a timeout of zero when I try to use the solver it returns ``Z3_L_UNDEF`` with the reason ( given by ``Z3_solver_get_reason_unknown()``) ``cancelled``.

Here's a simple demonstration. In ``fpa_example()`` in ``examples/c/test_capi.c`` add the following code just after creating the solver ``s``.

```
  // Set "no timeout" for some reason this causes
  // the solver to get "canceled"... what!?
  Z3_params solverParams = Z3_mk_params(ctx);
  Z3_params_inc_ref(ctx, solverParams);
  Z3_symbol timeoutParamStrSymbol = Z3_mk_string_symbol(ctx, "timeout");
  // "timeout (unsigned int) timeout (in milliseconds) (0 means no timeout) (default: 0)"
  Z3_params_set_uint(ctx,
                     solverParams,
                     timeoutParamStrSymbol,
                     0); // no timeout
  Z3_solver_set_params(ctx, s, solverParams);
  Z3_params_dec_ref(ctx, solverParams);
```

With this code added when running the example you see

```
FPA-example
c4: (and (= x_plus_y (fp.add rm x y))
     (= x_plus_y (fp #b0 #b10000000100 #x5000000000000))
     (not (= rm roundTowardZero))
     (not (fp.isZero y))
     (not (fp.isNaN y))
     (not (fp.isInfinite y)))
Error: canceled
```

As you can see the solver fails with reason ``canceled``.

When I set a non-zero timeout the solver seems to behave correctly. Although sometimes I get ``timeout`` and sometimes I get ``unknown`` back from ``Z3_solver_get_reason_unknown()`` which is a little annoying.

Any idea what is going on here? Is this a bug or am I using the API incorrectly?

issue_title: Active wagtail admin language is used to save translated fields on Page (eg. title, slug)
issue_body:
Wagtail admin uses `LocaleMiddleware` to detect browser language.

Imagine a custom page model translating the `title` field. When using the wagtail admin in eg German, the value set on the `title_de` field will also be written to the `title` field of `Page` (the wagtail built in root model) when saving. When using the admin in eg. English, the `Page.title` will contain the English value after saving.

When editors with different language operating systems/browsers use the admin (which is not uncommon when operating a multi-language site), the languages of the `title` fields of the various pages stored will vary.

This has a number of problematic side effects:
- The `Page.title` field is used in the list views of wagtail admin. So the list will show a mix of all kinds of languages and make it difficult for editors to find the articles they are looking for.
- Speaking of "finding things": when using the basic wagtail setup with DB search backend, the translated fields cannot be searched. This is a wagtail limitation, only fields on the `Page` model can be searched with this backend. 
  However, that would not be a problem, if the titles on the `Page` model would stay consistent eg. "always in English". Imagine using the word "district" in the title for eg a page describing a location: you'd have to search for "bezirk" if the last saving editor happened used a German browser, "quartier" if they were using French etc. You just never know what to search for.

The "translation overloaded" fields of `Page` should always contain the same language, probably best to use `settings.LANGUAGE_CODE`.

issue_body:
I am using wechatpy to add wechat login capability to a website, and I found that WeChat's servers refuse to accept the URLs generated by WeChatOAuth.qrconnect_url.

After some investigation, I found that this method does not encode URLs correctly.

WeChat servers will accept the URLs after changing:

        redirect_uri = quote(self.redirect_uri)

to

        redirect_uri = quote(self.redirect_uri, safe=u'')

Wanted to pass this along to the team and other users who are unable to present their users with the login QR code.
-gary

issue_title: Sentinel 3 SLSTR reading the solar/viewing angles fails
issue_body:
When using the `nc_slstr` reader the solar and viewing angles aren't loaded properly. 

Given a loaded SLSTR scene, the available datasets are:
```python
scn.available_dataset_names()

['S1', 'S2', 'S3', 'S4', 'S5', 'S6', 'S7', 'S8', 'S9', 'latitude', 'longitude', 
'satellite_azimuth_angle', 'satellite_zenith_angle', 'solar_azimuth_angle', 
'solar_zenith_angle']
```` 
But indexing the scene with `scn['solar_azimuth_angle']` results in a `KeyError`.


After digging around a bit I noticed three issues. 

First, there is a "mapper dictionary" for the datatset names  in the `NCSLSTRAngles` class which looks like:

```python
    datasets = {'satellite_azimuth_angle': 'satellite_azimuth_t' + view,
                'satellite_zenith_angle': 'satellite_zenith_t' + view,
                'solar_azimuth_angle': 'solar_azimuth_t' + view,
                'solar_zenith_angle': 'solar_zenith_t' + view}
```

Based on the SLSTR products I have this should be `sat_`, instead of `satellite_`:

```python
    datasets = {'satellite_azimuth_angle': 'sat_azimuth_t' + view,
                'satellite_zenith_angle': 'sat_zenith_t' + view,
                'solar_azimuth_angle': 'solar_azimuth_t' + view,
                'solar_zenith_angle': 'solar_zenith_t' + view}
```

Secondly, the `_FillValue` attribute doesn't exist for the `geometry_tn.nc`, and also not for the `cartesian_*.nc` products. The missing values have are filled with `NaN`, so this could be fixed with:

`variable.attrs.get('_FillValue', np.nan)`

That would still use the `_FillValue` attribute if it's present in the attributes of the dataset, and otherwise assume `np.nan`.


Lastly, the `NCSLSTRAngles` class returns what looks like a deprecated `Dataset` class, but SatPy seems to expect an `xarray` dataset. By peaking at the OLCI reader, I changed the old code:

```python
        proj = Dataset(values,
                       copy=False,
                       units=units,
                       platform_name=self.platform_name,
                       standard_name=variable.attrs['standard_name'],
                       sensor=self.sensor)
        return proj
```
to:
```python
        proj = xr.DataArray(da.from_array(values, chunks=(CHUNK_SIZE, CHUNK_SIZE)),
                            dims=['y', 'x'])

        proj.attrs['platform_name'] = self.platform_name
        proj.attrs['sensor'] = self.sensor

        proj.attrs.update(key.to_dict())

        return proj
```

I made a branch on my fork which has these changes implemented, but i'm not sure if i fixed everything conform the SatPy standards:
https://github.com/RutgerK/satpy/tree/fix_slstr

Additionally that branch also uses `os.path.sep` in the `yaml reader` which is necessary on Windows,  otherwise SatPy won't find any files:

These are some of the files I tried this on:
```
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180628T102931_20180628T103231_20180629T155259_0179_033_008_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180629T100320_20180629T100620_20180630T152011_0179_033_022_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180630T093709_20180630T094009_20180701T151640_0179_033_036_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
S3A_SL_1_RBT____20180702T102546_20180702T102846_20180703T150049_0179_033_065_2700_LN2_O_NT_003.SEN3
```

**Environment Info:**
 - OS: Window 10 64bit
 - SatPy Version: 0.9.0 (py35)
 - PyResample Version: 1.10.0  (py35)

issue_body:
The recommend way to use pyminiracer is:

```
>>> from py_mini_racer import py_mini_racer
>>> ctx = py_mini_racer.MiniRacer()
```

If we want to have several isolated contexts, the best way seems to create several contexts but it seems to dlopen the library each time a new context is created. Could pyminiracer dlopen the library only once in order to be more memory efficient while keeping the context isolated?

issue_title: pyotp/urllib.quote encoding for spaces incompatible with some apps
issue_body:
After doing some testing with the iOS apps `OTP Auth` and `LastPass Authenticator` I've come to notice these (and likely more) interpret the issuer string `Hello+World` to be **`Hello+World`** when they should be rendering as **`Hello World`**. pyotp currently encodes with the `urllib.quote()` method which uses the plus sign to encode spaces. When I changed the issuer string from `Hello+World` to `Hello%20World` **all** tested apps correctly understood this to be a space.

It should be noticed that the plus sign is only interpreted as a space in `application/x-www-form-encoded` content, while the true HTML encoding (as referenced by current TOTP standards) to be used with the issuer name uses `%20` as a space character.

Let me know if you have any questions regarding the matter.

issue_title: upcoef - TypeError: No matching signature found
issue_body:
I've come across this error when trying to reconstruct data from the coefficients given by dwt2 in a project to port some matlab code to python.  An example of the data and code that I'm using is given below.

`data = np.array([[ 1.47728539,  1.59481049,  1.59481049,  1.46021461,  1.44889259],
                            [ 1.48014069,  1.48872185,  1.67795181,  1.38459396,  1.32822609],
                            [ 1.41849327,  1.57595444,  1.50884819,  1.44937897,  1.57595444],
                            [ 1.46079826,  1.53956032,  1.54245186,  1.53089523,  1.49732971],
                            [ 1.49732971,  1.4323349 ,  1.44937897,  1.49158859,  1.48872185]])`

`ca, (_, _, _) = pywt.dwt2(data, 'db1')`

`reconstructed = pywt.upcoef('a', ca, 'db1')`

`---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-340-79d9bbb59e6c> in <module>()
----> 1 pywt.upcoef('a', ca, 'db1')

/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/pywt/_dwt.py in upcoef(part, coeffs, wavelet, level, take)
    341     if part not in 'ad':
    342         raise ValueError("Argument 1 must be 'a' or 'd', not '%s'." % part)
--> 343     return np.asarray(_upcoef(part == 'a', coeffs, wavelet, level, take))

/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/pywt/_extensions/_dwt.cpython-34m.so in pywt._extensions._dwt.__pyx_fused_cpdef (pywt/_extensions/_dwt.c:7860)()

TypeError: No matching signature found`

However, using idwt2 works and gives approximately the same answers compared to the matlab code that uses the native upcoef2 function.

`coeffs = (ca, (None, None, None))`

`reconstructed = pywt.idwt2(coeffs, 'db1')`

I think that this kind of TypeError is usually raised when there is a conflict between type definitions, but everything is of type numpy.ndarray and dtype float64.

issue_title: Define banned outcome based on code + message
issue_body:
As seen here: https://doc.scrapinghub.com/crawlera.html#errors

503 could mean multiple errors, not just a ban:
```
X-Crawlera-Error	Response Code	Error Message
...
noslaves	503	No available proxies
slavebanned	503	Website crawl ban
serverbusy	503	Server busy: too many outstanding requests
...
```
scrapy-crawlera only checks for the code, and could be misleading. 
  ```
      if response.status == self.ban_code:
            self._bans[key] += 1
            if self._bans[key] > self.maxbans:
                self.crawler.engine.close_spider(spider, 'banned')
            else:
                after = response.headers.get('retry-after')
                if after:
                    self._set_custom_delay(request, float(after))
            self.crawler.stats.inc_value('crawlera/response/banned')
        else:
```

IMHO it should consider the message of the response as well: HTTP code 503 and "Proxy has been banned" 

I discovered that this is the output of scrapy:
```
{'crawlera/request': 410730,
 'crawlera/request/method/GET': 410730,
 'crawlera/response': 410412,
 'crawlera/response/banned': 433,
 'crawlera/response/error': 48,
 'crawlera/response/error/banned': 15,
 'crawlera/response/error/internal_error': 15,
 'crawlera/response/error/timeout': 18,
 'crawlera/response/status/200': 409414,
 'crawlera/response/status/400': 514,
 'crawlera/response/status/403': 3,
 'crawlera/response/status/500': 15,
 'crawlera/response/status/502': 15,
 'crawlera/response/status/503': 433,
 'crawlera/response/status/504': 18,
```

and Crawlera stats show only 15 errors with 503 and "Proxy has been banned" which matches this count  `'crawlera/response/error/banned'`

issue_title: Unpinned warnings ignore --json
issue_body:
* safety version: 1.8.1
* Python version: 3.6.5
* Operating System: MacOSX

### Description

If a requirements.txt file has unpinned entries, parsing output with `--json` is difficult, as the warnings are not in the data structure.

### What I Did

I ran `safety check --json -r ./requirements.txt` against a requirements file that had unpinned entries, and received some warnings, as expected. However the warnings were logged to stdout, outside the json structure, meaning the tools couldn't safely parse the output:

```
Warning: unpinned requirement 'simplejson' found in ./requirements.txt, unable to check.
[]
```

Perhaps these should be in the json, with the module name, perhaps empty version strings, and the description field containing the warning message.

issue_title: JWT bad check for the nbf claim
issue_body:
When decoding a token that has the nbf claim set the library fails to decode it with:

    (...)
    File "(...)/.tox/env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jwcrypto/jwt.py", line 343, in _check_default_claims
        self._check_exp(claims['nbf'], time.time(), self._leeway)
    File "(...)/.tox/env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jwcrypto/jwt.py", line 323, in _check_exp
    claim, limit, leeway))
    JWTExpired: Expired at 1480353842, time: 1480353902(leeway: 60)

Which refers to this line:

    self._check_exp(claims['nbf'], time.time(), self._leeway)

https://github.com/latchset/jwcrypto/blob/b81d7ea6459b2ec42da519ba19f983e2f4416fbb/jwcrypto/jwt.py#L343

As you can see, the nbf is checked using the _check_exp(), I think it should be checked using the _check_nbf():

https://github.com/latchset/jwcrypto/blob/b81d7ea6459b2ec42da519ba19f983e2f4416fbb/jwcrypto/jwt.py#L325

Thanks

issue_title: Backslashes get stripped from regexes used in transcript testing
issue_body: With the way parsing in **Cmd2TestCase** is implemented, it means that any transcript with regular expressions containing backslashes will not behave as expected. Backslashes in regexes are very common.  It would be much better if a much wider variety of regexes worked properly.

issue_title: set and show commands are largely redundant
issue_body:
The **set** command automatically calls the **show** command if called without any arguments. 

Thus, the show command is largely superfluous.  The current functionality present within show could still exist as a helper method, it just doesn't need to be its own separate command.

issue_title: RandomUnderSampler oversamples
issue_body:
In multi-class problems, the RandomUnderSampler will oversample the intermediary (non-majority, non-minority) classes when a small ratio is used. This also results in an error when "replacement" is set to "False".

I have the case of three classes, with the following counts:
A = 200, B = 50, C = 10

With ratio=0.1 (and replacement=True), I expected to end up with:
A = 100, B = 50, C = 10

But instead I end up with:
A = 100, B = 100, C = 10

In other words, it is oversampling. Of course, this will result in an error when I set replacement=False.

1. Is oversampling in this case the intended behavior?
2. If not, it can be fixed by changing (the size parameter) in line 119-120 to something like:
`            indx = random_state.choice(indx, size=min(self.stats_c_[key], num_samples),   replace=self.replacement)`

issue_title: Do not swallow Sentry capture's result

issue_title: Apache SoftwareCollections
issue_body: It might be useful to allow the script to detect if `scl enable httpd24 /bin/bash` is applied when searching for DocRoot.  As it stands, the script doesn't allow for installation when the default apache package is `httpd24-httpd`, rather than `httpd` on RHEL7/CentOS7.  It looks for vhosts in `/etc/httpd/conf.d/*` for `ServerName` and/or `ServerAlias`.  However, when httpd24-httpd from SoftwareCollections is used, those are specified in `/opt/rh/httpd24/root/etc/httpd/conf.d`.

issue_title: DGPS Inject & forwarding packet loss
issue_body:
Hi,

I have the following setup:
- An Intel Edison with Yocto running Mavproxy.
- It has an RFD900 (868 in fact) modem on the serial port
- It runs a wifi hotspot
- It runs a DGPS base station, serving out RTCM3 via TCP on port 9000.

I can just run mavproxy with `--out=udpbcast:192.168.42.255:14550` and every device that runs QGCS on the hotspot will be able to connect to the drone. Yay!

However, as soon as I start to inject DGPS packages the connected device get a connection loss every second second. Either with `>>module load DGPS_TCP` or with `--load-module DGPS_TCP`

The official DGPS code in this repo was UDP an thus unusable, so I had to adapt it to TCP for my dgps server. My code is here:
https://github.com/antonvh/MAVProxy/blob/reach_inject/MAVProxy/modules/mavproxy_DGPS_TCP.py

How can I start to find out where the intermittent connection loss is coming from?

issue_title: Use balance_hash in balance proof
issue_body:
Use `balance_hash` in the balance proof - http://raiden-network-specification.readthedocs.io/en/latest/messaging.html#balance-proof. 


- Add `locked_amount`, `balance_hash` to all balance proof-related messages & states. These can be set to `0` / `b''` initially or included in duplicated functions.

Some related files:
- `raiden/encoding/messages.py`
- `transfer/balance_proof.py`
- `transfer/state.py` - `BalanceProofSignedState`, `balanceproof_from_envelope`
- `raiden/messages.py` - `EnvelopeMessage`
- `raiden/raiden_event_handler` - `handle_contract_channelclose`, `handle_contract_channelupdate`
- `handle_contract_channelsettle` needs to provide all balance proof data to the smart contract now.


### ToDo
- [x] Add locked_amount to signature #1558
- [ ] Add balance_hash to the balance proof

issue_title: Integrate raiden-libs to avoid duplicating code and effort
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-libs was created to aggregate common tools used across `raiden-network` repositories.
It should be integrated in `raiden` to avoid duplicating code and effort. E.g. https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-libs/issues/50

issue_title: `removedb` sanity checks assumes incorrect prefix
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The raiden db format spec changed after the directory sanity check was introduced. Now the user always get's warnings about the raiden datadir not being a raiden datadir:

```
$ ls /home/konrad/.raiden/
9605b58f  fadc801b

$ dist/raiden--x86_64.AppImage removedb
WARNING: The specified directory does not appear to be a Raiden data directory.
Are you sure you want to delete /home/konrad/.raiden? [y/N]:
```

## Solution

Fix the sanity check at https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/ui/cli.py#L626 to understand the new format

issue_title: Control number of jobs in case of nested workflow.
issue_body:
Right now `sos_run` observes `-j` option independent of the master process. So running a subworkflow in multi-processing mode essentially does

```
J1
J2
J3 --> sos_run --> J5, J6, J7, J8
J4 
```

It should in theory have a process pool to which master and nested workflows send jobs to.

issue_title: Concurrent processes for non-task actions
issue_body: I'm running into various issues with `task` that I should report soon. But still I think for computations running on local desktop I'd rather not use `task` because it is otherwise much simpler to manage (I'll not have to worry about status or zombie processes). That'd essentially be what we have before (in the last release). The issue now is that without task I can only run it as a single thread process. I'm wondering if we can easily allow for multiple thread in that case -- basically ask non-task jobs to honor `-j`.

issue_title: BUG: The torchvision.models.alexnet is incorrect
issue_body:
In [self.classifier](https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/master/torchvision/models/alexnet.py#L32), the correct order of layers should be fc6-relu-dropout-fc7-relu-dropout-fc8, which means that dropout layer should be after the fc and relu layers.

This does not matter when inference. However, when you fine tune AlexNet model on other datasets, the incorrect layer sequence will result in an accuracy drop of 2 percents or more.

Can I make a PR to fix it? The pre-trained model URL need to update too.

issue_title: Support class-based views
issue_body:
I wrote some of the code to do this in a branch https://github.com/python-security/pyt/compare/class_based_views, but since I'm working on other things and this feature seems cool and important I'm making this issue 👍 

Let me know if you would like any help in implementing.

issue_title: Login timer lists ms
issue_body:
Please be sure to read through other issues as well to make sure what you are suggesting/reporting has not already
been suggested/reported

### Type:

- [ ] Suggestion
- [x] Bug

### Brief description of the problem
I may be wrong here, but I'm fairly certain the login time listed in the console window is incorrectly listing milliseconds (ms) instead of seconds (s)
### Expected behavior
If my assumption is correct, it should list the value with an s at the end like (4.95812s)
### Actual behavior
Time listed after logging into Discord is instead (4.95812ms)
### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch Red
2. Watch the console
3. Check the time it lists that it took to log into discord
4. This process usually takes 3-5 seconds for me but I always see the listing as ms

issue_title: [V3 Downloader] Repo add error on bad cog
issue_body:
# Command bugs

<!-- 
Did you find a bug with a command? Fill out the following:
-->

#### Command name

`repo add`

#### What cog is this command from?

Downloader

#### What were you expecting to happen?

Properly add a repo

#### What actually happened?

Repo entered an error state where files were downloaded but repo was not added

#### How can we reproduce this issue?

`repo add` on a repo with a cog that can't be imported due to an error (such as syntax)

issue_title: Trouble running spython built as singularity image
issue_body:
Hello,

It is quite possible I'm going about something wrong (I'm very new to Singularity): after grabbing the `Singularity` recipe file from this rep and building an image (`sudo singularity build spython Singularity`) based on it, I wind up with what appears to be a `spython` executable image (I prefer this term to container for singularity ;-)):

[beb82@euca-128-84-10-138 ~]$ du -h -s spython

issue_title: Consolidate and build high quality command line tooling (master task)
issue_body:
The command line tooling in dagster is a mess. This should be consolidated into a single, sensible command that provides a lot of value.

Initially I built this that you treated a file that contained the definition of a pipeline as a command line tool. This was done by conditionally invoking the pipeline if it was main. This was a mistake. We want to be able to invoke a pipeline from a traditional feeling command line tool.

embedded_cli.py is the code that drives this so-called "embedded" cli.

To see an example of this:

run 

python3 pipelines.py in dagster/dagster_examples

That is the multi-pipeline case.

Or, for example

python3 pipeline.py in dagster/dagster_examples/qhp for example

That is the single-pipeline case

This should be sub-divided into a number of tasks:

- [X]  File layout for making dagster pipeline tool aware of pipelines. This is essentially already implemented. dagster.cli.__init__.py:12-25
- [ ] Listing pipelines and their description (also these lines, but should be improved)
- [ ] A *good* way of printing out pipeline metadata. Currently print_pipeline in embedded_cli.py. It is *not* good. Invoked using the "meta" command in the single pipeline case.
- [X] Consume a yaml file that constructs the objects defined in dagster/config.py. We want to be able to drive a pipeline execution or materialization from a yaml file.
- [ ] Be able to *codegen* a yaml file based on the declared definition of a pipeline.
- [ ] Be able to verify the validity of an incoming yaml file based on a definition of a pipeline (not hi-pri)

                                      'col5': [-4, -5, -6, -7]})

In [5]: modin_df = pd.DataFrame(pandas_df)

In [6]: for k, v in modin_df.groupby(by=[1,2,1,2]):
    print(k)
    print(v)

issue_title: collectd.__version__ and collectd.__version_info__ should exist
issue_body:
We should be able to say

    >>> import collectd
    >>> collectd.__version__
    '1.0.2'
    >>> collectd.__version_info__
    (1, 0, 2, 'final', 0)
